task_name: Implement Local Live Builder Adapter Readiness Candidate Mock
goal: Implement a local deterministic Live Builder Adapter Readiness Candidate mock that consumes the BP-072 handoff envelope and emits a local readiness output without calling Builder, creating tasks, configuring auth, reading secrets, using persistence, or executing anything.
claims: BP-073 implements a local Live Builder Adapter Readiness Candidate mock from the BP-072 handoff envelope.
BP-073 blocks live Builder calls, task creation, execution, adapter configuration, auth, secrets, persistence, and approval recording.
BP-073 does not call Builder, create tasks, execute tasks, configure auth, use secrets, use DB, persist data, or record approval.
assumptions: The BP-072 contract is the source of truth for Live Builder Adapter Readiness input and output envelopes.
The first live-builder-adapter readiness implementation should be local and fixture-reviewed before any Builder adapter is connected.
Live Builder, Builder Task Create, and Execute remain blocked after BP-073.
dod: examples/live-builder-adapter-readiness-candidate contains golden input/output fixtures for prepared, review, and blocked cases.
tools/test-live-builder-adapter-readiness-candidate-fixtures.cjs compares CLI output against golden fixtures.
stop_conditions: Live Builder access, Builder Task Create, adapter configuration, Auth, secrets, persistence, approval recording, Execute, Approve, Push, Deploy, or DB becomes necessary
Live Builder Adapter Readiness mock needs to write target files instead of only returning an envelope
prior_task_findings: BP-072 defined a document-only Live Builder Adapter Readiness Contract and named a local candidate mock with fixtures as the next safe step.
